calendar.matters:
MOTION TO AMEND/MODIFY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-28-2019 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-28-2019 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned by Department 514 for Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Billion Micro, Inc., a Nevada corporation, and Mongkol Jaemjaeng and Chonthida S. Jaemjaeng, in their offical and individual capacities.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185. Telephone: 415-551-0334. Email: aldegado@sftc.org.

Robert W. Ottinger, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Prayoot Jantaradejduangdee, an individual.
Anna Tom, Class Action Damage Analysis for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Billion Micro, Inc., a Nevada corporation, Mongkol Jaemjaeng or Chonthida S. Jaemjaeng.
Plaintiff's complaint was filed October 29, 2019.
Defendants Billion Micro, Inc., Mongkol Jaemjaeng and Chonthida S. Jaemjaeng filed their answer to plaintiff's complaint on December 5, 2018.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was served on March 15, 2019, setting trial for today, October 28, 2019.
No DOE defendants were named in plaintiff's complaint.
Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions as to defendant Mongkol Jaemjaeng was GRANTED October 18, 2019, and defendant's answer was STRICKEN.
The Court DENIED plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions as to defendants Billion Micro, Inc. and Chonthida S. Jaemjaeng.

The Court makes a finding that defendants Billion Micro, Inc., a Nevada corporation, and Mongkol Jaemjaeng and Chonthida S. Jaemjaeng, in their offical and individual capacities, have voluntarily absented themselves from today's proceedings and do not wish to contest the allegations set for in plaintiff's complaint.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Prayoot Jantaradejduangdee, an individual, and against defendants Billion Micro, Inc., a Nevada corporation, and Mongkol Jaemjaeng and Chonthida S. Jaemjaeng, in their offical and individual capacities, as follows:

Principal/Damages - $2,066,033.40.
Perjudgment Interest - 416,008.00.
Attorney Fees - Per Motion.
Costs - Per Memorandum of Costs.
Total - $2,482,041.40.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Plaintiff Prayoot Jantaradejduangdee'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty Robert W. Ottinger of Ottinger Employment Lawyers t: 415.463.1283; No other appearances.

RULING - ARGUED, Plaintiff Prayoot Jantaradejduangdee'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions is granted. Mongkol Jaemjaeng's answer is stricken and the court enters a default. The motion for terminating sanctions is denied as to Chonthida S. Jaemjaeng and Billion Micro, Inc. Prevailing party to prepare Order. Proceedings reported by David Disberg CSR#7768 t: 415.298.1538; Judge: Katharine Gallo; Clerk: Johnny Sengmany (jpt/302).

PLAINTIFF PRAYOOT JANTARADEJDUANGDEE's Motion For Terminating Sanctions.

Transferred to be heard on the Discovery Calendar at 9:00 am, in Room 301, on the same date.

Plaintiff Prayoot Jantaradejduangdee'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-04-2019 Continued To Oct-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert Ottinger, Esq. of the Ottinger Firm, PC, T:415.262.0096, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court modified its tentative ruling on Plaintiff Prayoot Jantaradejduangdee'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions, as follows:

On September 27, 2019, the court filed its order granting the attorneys for Micro Billion, Inc. Mongkol Jaemjaeng and Chonthida Jaemjaeng to withdraw as counsel of record. This motion is continued to October 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301 to give the defendants time to retain new counsel and prepare for the motion.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order. Plaintiff's counsel to serve order on both defendants and defendants' former counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

And Mortion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Defendant Monkol Jaemjaeng
Off Calendar Sep-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Robert Ottinger of Ottinger employment Lawyer's 415-463-1283 appeared for plaintiff. / Atty. John P. Danelon of Ellis & Abouelsood 925-726-4529 appeared for defendants.

Hearing Held - PLAINTIFF PRAYOOT JANTARADEJDUANGDEE'S Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Defendant Monkol Jaemjaeng. The Court rules that Parties should meet and confer within two court days to have the day, time and place for desposition. Deposition should take place before September 27, 2019. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge Pro Tem William Lynn, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)
